goals: Distribute NVST as standalone executables (no Node/npm/Bun required on the user machine).
Simplify installation and updates via a single executable per platform.
Produce and publish binaries for macOS, Linux, and Windows when a release (tag) is created, matching the current tag-based release flow.

userStories.title: Build produces one binary per target platform
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want to build a single executable with Bun for a given platform so that I can ship one file per OS.

acceptanceCriteria.text: A Bun build (or equivalent) produces one binary (e.g. `nvst` or `nvst.exe`) for the current or specified platform.
Build is reproducible from the repo (documented script or command, e.g. `bun run build:binary` or `bun build src/cli.ts --outdir=dist`).

userStories.title: Built binary behaves like `bun nvst`
userStories.description: As an end user, I want the downloaded binary to behave like `bun nvst` so that I get the same CLI and behavior without Bun.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Running the binary (e.g. `./nvst --help` or `nvst.exe --help`) shows the same help and subcommands as `bun nvst`.
At least one non-trivial command (e.g. `nvst define requirement` or `nvst --version`) runs successfully when invoked via the binary.

userStories.title: Single entry point to build all three platform binaries
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want one script or command that produces binaries for macOS, Linux, and Windows so that releases can be built consistently.

acceptanceCriteria.text: One script or command (e.g. `bun run build:binaries` or a small script in `scripts/`) builds executables for mac, linux, and windows (e.g. from a single runner using Bun’s target or matrix).

userStories.title: Binary is self-contained (no Bun/Node on target machine)
userStories.description: As an end user, I want to run the binary on a machine without Bun or Node installed so that I can use NVST with a single download.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The built binary runs on a clean macOS, Linux, or Windows system without Bun or Node.js installed (e.g. verified in a minimal container or VM, or documented as a requirement of the build).
No runtime error due to missing Bun/Node when invoking the binary.

userStories.title: GitHub Actions workflow builds and publishes binaries on release
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes the three binaries when a release is created so that distribution stays aligned with the current tag-based release flow.

acceptanceCriteria.text: A workflow (new or updated) runs when a GitHub Release is published (same trigger as current publish flow, e.g. `release: types: [published]`).
The workflow builds binaries for macOS, Linux, and Windows (e.g. via matrix or Bun native targets).
The resulting binaries are attached to the GitHub Release as assets (e.g. `nvst-darwin-amd64`, `nvst-linux-amd64`, `nvst-windows-amd64.exe` or similar).

functionalRequirements.description: The CLI entry point remains `src/cli.ts`; the Bun build compiles it (and its dependencies) into a single executable.
Binaries are produced for at least: macOS (darwin), Linux (linux), Windows (windows), with architecture (e.g. x64) implied or explicit as needed.
Release artifacts (binaries) are generated only when a release is published (tag/release trigger), not on every push.
The project remains CLI-only; no change to the set of commands or to AGENTS.md/PROJECT_CONTEXT.md beyond distribution and release steps.
